checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully represents the text content of all six pages of the source PDF. I have compared each section ('How to enroll for MFA', 'Authenticator', 'Modify MFA enrollment', 'Change default MFA enrollment method', 'Delete MFA method', 'Enroll MFA via an application') against the source document. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or instructions were found. The metadata and AI-generated alt text for images are within acceptable parameters.

The only URL present in the source document is 'https://myncid.nc.gov/', which appears on pages 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5. This URL is correctly transcribed in every instance in the output JSON. No fabricated phone numbers, email addresses, or other URLs were introduced.

All six pages of the source PDF are accounted for in the output JSON. The 'metadata' correctly identifies a 'page_count' of 6, and the 'sections' array contains content corresponding to every major heading and step-by-step instruction set found in the source document.
The source document does not contain any tables; the content is primarily structured as headings, lists, and images (buttons/screenshots). Therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: I verified the step-by-step instructions for all MFA enrollment and modification methods. The output includes all steps (1-6 for Email, Text, Phone; 1-9 for Authenticator, etc.) and the critical warning notes (e.g., the note on page 5 regarding deleting a default MFA method). No significant text omissions were detected.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output matches the wording in the source document. For example, the specific phrasing '8 digit' (without the word 'code') on page 6, step 3 is correctly preserved in the output. Punctuation and bold/link formatting are also accurately reflected.

checks.a_analysis: The output correctly handles the repetitive page headers ('MyNCID User Guide for MFA' and 'How to enroll for MFA:') found in the source PDF by representing them only once in the logical structure of the document sections. No unintentional duplication of content blocks was found.

The heading hierarchy is generally logical. The main title is H1, major tasks like 'How to enroll for MFA:' are H2, and sub-methods (Email, Text Message, Phone Call) are H3. While 'Authenticator' is promoted to a Section Title (effectively H2) rather than remaining H3 under the enrollment heading, this is an acceptable adjustment for a multi-page document structure.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, this check is not applicable.

On Page 5 ('Delete MFA method:'), the source PDF contains a prominent 'Note' box at the top of the page, intended to be read before the steps. In the output JSON, this note is placed at the very end of the section, after all instructions. This reordering significantly changes the logical flow and context of the instructions.
checks.b_evidence_source: Note: You cannot delete a MFA method if it is set as default. If you only have one MFA method, you will have to add a new one and set it as default to be able to delete the old method
checks.b_evidence_output: Note: You cannot delete a MFA method if it is set as default. If you only have one MFA method, you will have to add a new one and set it as default to be able to delete the old method

checks.a_analysis: Throughout the document, numbered lists (1-6, 1-9, etc.) are pervasive and systematically fragmented. Every time an image or button occurs within a list sequence, the list structure is broken into a new, separate list object. This results in numerous single-item lists and the loss of correct sequence/numbering semantics (e.g., Step 4 becoming item 1 of a new list).
checks.b_evidence_source: 1. Login... 2. Click... 3. Click... [Image] 4. Select... [Image] 5. Verify... [Image] 6. Enter...
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'Email' section is broken into four separate 'list' objects (one with 3 items, and three with 1 item each), separated by 'image' elements.

checks.a_analysis: The URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' appears multiple times across all instructions in the source PDF. In the output JSON, these are converted only as plain text runs (with underlined styling) but are not logically represented as clickable links (missing URL/link metadata), failing the accessibility requirement for these to be clickable.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document in step 1 of various lists

checks.a_analysis: In the 'Text Message' section of the first page, the visual organization and flow of information is significantly broken. The sequence of buttons does not match the instructions: step 3 should be 'ADD ENROLLMENT' but shows 'NEXT', and step 4 should be 'ADD' but shows 'SUBMIT'. Subsequent steps are followed by large green placeholder boxes. This rearrangement of content blocks and broken visual sequence disrupts the user's ability to follow the instructions correctly.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1, Text Message section: Step 3 has 'ADD ENROLLMENT' button; Step 4 has 'ADD' button; Step 5 has 'NEXT' button; Step 6 has 'SUBMIT' button.
checks.b_evidence_output: HTML output, Text Message section: Step 3 is followed by 'NEXT' button; Step 4 is followed by 'SUBMIT' button; Steps 5 and 6 are followed by large green rectangles.
checks.b_evidence_location: How to enroll for MFA: Text Message section

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables.

Numerous meaningful images, icons, and buttons are missing or broken in the HTML output. Page 2 shows three large green rectangles instead of the Authenticator logo, QR scanning diagram, and success dialog. Page 3 is missing the pencil edit icons next to 'email' and 'phone'. Page 4 is missing the radio button ('Set as default') and checkbox ('Make as default') images. Page 5 is missing the trash can icon. Additionally, nearly all action buttons (SEND OTP, VERIFY, REMOVE, SUBMIT) from pages 3 through 6 are completely absent from the rendered HTML.
checks.b_evidence_source: Meaningful icons and images on pages 2 (Simeio logo, QR scanning, Success box), 3 (pencil icon), 4 (radio button, checkbox), and 5 (trash icon), plus various action buttons throughout.
checks.b_evidence_output: HTML output shows green boxes for images on page 2 and entirely omits the icons and buttons mentioned above in the corresponding sections.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections for Authenticator (Page 2), Modify MFA (Page 3), Change default (Page 4), and Delete MFA (Page 5)

checks.a_analysis: There are no instances of content blocks being visually duplicated in the HTML output compared to the source PDF.
